title: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Amazon operates two world-class businesses: AWS (the cloud computing leader with 30%+ margins) and e-commerce (the logistics moat that competitors can't replicate). AWS alone would be worth $1T+ as a ...

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC reflects Amazon.com Inc.'s risk profile, including sector-specific volatility, competitive dynamics, and macroeconomic sensitivity.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate assumes Amazon.com Inc. grows roughly in line with nominal GDP into perpetuity, reflecting the law of large numbers for a mature large-cap enterprise.

thesisTitle: The Everything Tax on Commerce and Cloud
thesisParagraphs: Amazon operates two world-class businesses: AWS (the cloud computing leader with 30%+ margins) and e-commerce (the logistics moat that competitors can't replicate). AWS alone would be worth $1T+ as a standalone entity, and Amazon's advertising business is quietly becoming a $60B+ revenue machine.

The challenge is capex intensity. Amazon is spending $75B+ annually on infrastructure, and FCF has historically been volatile due to investment cycles. My 15% growth rate reflects AWS's continued dominance and advertising growth, but the 10% discount rate accounts for the capital-intensive nature of the business and regulatory risks in both the US and EU.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How does Opus value AWS separately?
faq.answer: While this is a consolidated DCF, AWS contributes roughly 60-70% of Amazon's operating income on only 17% of revenue. At 25x operating income, AWS alone is worth approximately $1.2T, which represents about 55% of Amazon's current market cap.

faq.question: Why a 10% discount rate for Amazon?
faq.answer: Amazon's massive capex requirements ($75B+ annually), volatile FCF history, and exposure to regulatory risk across multiple jurisdictions (antitrust, labor, data privacy) justify a higher discount rate than typical large-cap tech.

faq.question: Is Amazon's advertising business underappreciated?
faq.answer: Yes. Amazon Ads generates $60B+ in near-100% margin revenue, growing 20%+ annually. It's arguably the most undervalued segment of any mega-cap tech company.
